name: Hills Bank and Trust Company
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering checking, savings, mortgages, and wealth management services with a focus on local banking relationships and agricultural lending in Eastern Iowa.
description_long: Hills Bank and Trust Company is a traditional community bank serving Eastern Iowa with FDIC insurance backing all deposit accounts. The bank has established itself as a relationship-focused financial institution, emphasizing personalized service through in-person appointments and local branch locations. Based on their website content, they maintain a strong commitment to agricultural and small business lending, as evidenced by their educational content addressing farm family planning and beginning farmer growth strategies. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking services including free checking accounts with no minimum balance requirements, savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and personal loan products. Their mortgage division provides home purchase and refinancing services with a dedicated local lending team. Hills Bank also operates a Wealth Management division handling investment advisory and retirement planning services. Additional services include online and mobile banking with features like mobile check deposit, card controls, and digital wallet integration (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay). What distinguishes Hills Bank is their emphasis on community education and local relationship banking. They host free seminars on first-time homebuying, budgeting, and debt management. Their content demonstrates specialized knowledge in agricultural finance and farm family economics. The bank also emphasizes fraud awareness education and provides tools like card controls for lost cards managed through their online banking platform. Routing number 073913755 is prominently displayed, indicating established banking infrastructure. Hills Bank is best suited for customers prioritizing traditional banking relationships, local service, and community connections over fintech innovation. However, the website provides no information about mobile app sophistication, interest rate competitiveness, or fees for specific services beyond free checking. No details are disclosed about business account minimums, loan terms, or investment product specifics. For consumers seeking high-yield savings or cutting-edge digital banking, alternative options may warrant comparison. Their strength lies in personalized service and agricultural/small business lending rather than technology-first banking.
services: Free checking accounts with no minimum balance
Savings accounts and certificates of deposit (CDs)
Personal loans and home equity loans/lines of credit
Wealth Management and investment advisory services
Online and mobile banking with 24/7 account access
Mobile wallet integration (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay)
Card controls for lost/compromised debit cards
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Free checking with no minimum balance requirements and unlimited debit card transactions
Free educational seminars on first-time homebuying, budgeting, and debt management
Local mortgage team providing in-person guidance for home purchase and refinance
Specialized agricultural lending expertise demonstrated through farm family content
Mobile banking with check deposit, card controls, and mobile wallet support
24/7 online account access through Hills Bank Online platform
cons: Website does not disclose interest rates for checking, savings, or CD products
No information about account fees, overdraft policies, or service charges beyond free checking
Limited transparency on mortgage rates and personal loan terms; must contact for pricing
No clear information about minimum balances for savings accounts, CDs, or business accounts
Website does not indicate digital banking innovation or competitive positioning versus regional/national banks
best_for: Iowa farmers and agricultural business owners seeking specialized lending expertise
First-time homebuyers wanting local mortgage guidance and free educational seminars
Small business owners in Eastern Iowa prioritizing relationship-based banking
Customers seeking traditional community banking with in-person branch relationships
diagnosis: Hills Bank is best for Eastern Iowa residents and agricultural business owners seeking personalized, relationship-based banking with strong local expertise. The primary caveat is that the website lacks transparency on interest rates, fees, and competitive product terms—requiring direct contact with the bank for pricing decisions and detailed account comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured community bank offering checking, savings, mortgages, and wealth management with focus on local relationships in Eastern Iowa.
